name: Maryland Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners for Audiologists, Hearing Aid Dispensers, Speech-Language Pathologists, and Music Therapists

address.description: Maryland Board of Audiologists, Hearing Aid Dispensers, and Speech-Language Pathologists, 4201 Patterson Avenue, Baltimore, Maryland 21215-2299

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Maryland SLP license cost?
Is Maryland part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Maryland allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Maryland have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Maryland participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Maryland without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Maryland?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
